item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ya’ll are so fantastic at writing my Aries as she is in the SCP universe. I can’t say anything for anyone else’s characters with regards to how they feel about how they’re characterized, but Aries has been pretty consistently great.
Also that fucking memory loss. Shit. I’ve been there before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
spoiler response
– hide block

Yeah, that's totally what I was going for - all the scientists contributed their own stuff, accidentally building something together that any individual would be fundamentally incapable of figuring out themselves because of the mental blocks installed by the bioweapon

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It makes me giddy to see Emmy pop up here again. ^_^

+ minor spoilers for 2871
- mostly an author headcanon thing:

The bit Emmy mentions syncs perfectly with something that I had in mind while writing 2871: its a product of the blind men and the elephant. None of the people working on it could explain how it works, because they each could only "see" part of how it really worked. They sort of built it around a 2871-shaped hole in each of their worldviews, with each having a piece of the puzzle that fit into that blank.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I thought this dragged in some places, and had some confusing bits, the writing remained exceptional throughout. Great ending to a great series (and you write Sigma-66 so well).

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hot damn that was a good read. You conveyed loss of memory very well, and that was one hell of a plot twist.

+1

item.title: oof ow the nebulous space behind my eyes
item.content:encoded:
haha we're all fucked

This is the finale for Team Fuhgeddaboutit, as part of Doomcon. We hope you've enjoyed everything so far, and that this tale is part of that enjoyment. And check out the epilogue, Oceanus, Father to All, when you're done.

This tale goes through a bunch of moods, so I can't recommend specific listening, but maybe start out with something like Justice and then move to like the soundtrack to It Follows when things start going tits up. That's what I did, plus a bunch more stuff.

Apologies to any who grated on the dialogue punctuation - I get repeatedly confused on the different guides for EU and US punctuation, so there's likely a fair bit of inconsistency here. We'll be doing a pass to clean that stuff up in the coming days, since grammar counts as minor edits.

Thanks to LordStonefish, WrongJohnSilver and especially Cyantreuse for putting up with me for the past month and a half as I stumbled through trying to get this done while IRL shit crumbled around me. It's been something.

Merry doomsmas to all, and to all a good night.
